notes: The design-rationale-comment assertion is the most discriminating: with_skill passes 100%, without_skill passes 0% — this is the clearest signal of skill value.
meta-tags assertion (OG + description) also discriminates strongly: with_skill 100%, without_skill fails on 2/3 evals.
semantic-html assertion discriminates on SaaS hero (without_skill missing <header>/<main>) but baseline portfolio/pricing happened to pass — moderate discriminator.
The portfolio eval shows the weakest differentiation (10 vs 9/10) because a solo Claude naturally produces good portfolio pages — the skill's extra value here is mostly documentation and a11y depth.
Timing data is noisy due to agents being blocked on permissions and outputs generated by orchestrator — not reliable for time comparisons this iteration.
Recommendation: add assertions for ARIA landmarks, OG tags specifically, and skip-to-content link to better capture the skill's accessibility improvements.
